title: Model Evaluation Quiz
description: Check your understanding of metrics, validation, and common pitfalls.

questions.question: Which metric would you optimize for an imbalanced fraud dataset where false negatives are very costly?
questions.explanation: Recall measures how many actual positives you captured—critical when missing a fraud case is expensive.

questions.question: Why do we avoid shuffling when validating time-series models?
options: Future information could leak into the past
It reduces the number of folds
questions.explanation: Shuffling mixes future records into the past, creating leakage that inflates metrics.

questions.question: What does ROC-AUC measure?
options: Probability the classifier ranks a positive higher than a negative
questions.explanation: ROC-AUC is the probability that a random positive receives a higher score than a random negative.

questions.question: How can you detect overfitting when running k-fold CV?
options: Validation scores vary widely across folds
questions.explanation: Large variance between folds indicates the model is overly sensitive to the training data splits.

questions.question: You tuned hyperparameters on the validation set. What should you report to stakeholders?
options: Results on the held-out test set
questions.explanation: Only the final evaluation on the untouched test set reflects real-world performance after tuning.
